skill.name: referral-program
skill.description: Design, optimize, and analyze referral programs, affiliate programs, ambassador programs, and word-of-mouth growth systems.
skill.summary: Create practical referral and affiliate programs with incentives, tracking, launch plans, and optimization guidance.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators, but review found these were Markdown diagrams, formulas, templates, and ordinary marketing terms. No executable code, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md. A low content concern remains because the skill claims access to referral data and third-party tools without implementation evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged As Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences and examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations contain diagrams, formulas, or email templates, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged line is inside Markdown instructional content or marks a fenced block boundary. No command invocation syntax is used outside documentation examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, but no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm use appears at the cited locations. The hits are ordinary referral-program words and headings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operations at these lines. The repeated hits align with substring matches in words such as design, codes, and strategy.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported system and network reconnaissance, but the cited lines discuss referral strategy, sharing, gamification, costs, and rewards. No host, service, or network discovery behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only marketing guidance and checklists near these locations. There is no scan command, network request, probing workflow, or system inventory instruction.

low_findings.title: Overbroad Claim Of External Data And Tool Access
low_findings.description: The skill states that the assistant has access to referral program data and third-party tools. No implementation supports this claim, but it could mislead users about available or authorized access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The statement is directly present, but there is no code that performs data or tool access. The risk is marketplace content accuracy rather than malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Referral and affiliate programs fail when incentives, timing, tracking, and fraud controls are unclear. This skill helps teams design launch-ready programs and improve existing ones with practical marketing frameworks.
actual_capabilities: Compares customer referral, affiliate, ambassador, and hybrid program models.
Designs incentive structures using customer value, margins, and target acquisition cost.
Maps referral loops, trigger moments, share mechanisms, and conversion paths.
Creates affiliate recruitment, enablement, launch, and email sequence guidance.
Defines measurement plans for referral rate, viral coefficient, CAC, LTV, and ROI.
Identifies referral fraud risks and practical prevention controls.
limitations: Does not connect to referral platforms or customer databases by itself.
Does not replace legal review of affiliate terms, tax rules, or payout policies.
Uses general marketing benchmarks that may not match every market or product.
Requires user-provided business metrics for accurate incentive sizing.

use_cases.title: Launch A Customer Referral Program
use_cases.description: Create a double-sided referral offer, sharing flow, launch checklist, and first email sequence for existing customers.

use_cases.title: Build An Affiliate Partner Program
use_cases.description: Define commissions, cookie windows, partner profiles, enablement assets, and outreach messages for external promoters.

use_cases.title: Improve Program Performance
use_cases.description: Diagnose low referral rates, poor conversion, weak incentives, and fraud risk using structured optimization prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Choose The Right Program Type
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new program
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between a customer referral program, affiliate program, or hybrid model. My product is [product], audience is [audience], LTV is [LTV], and CAC is [CAC].

prompt_templates.title: Design Referral Incentives
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a referral incentive structure for [business]. Use my LTV, gross margin, and target CAC to recommend reward type, reward size, and payout timing.

prompt_templates.title: Create An Affiliate Launch Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an affiliate program launch plan for [product]. Include commission structure, partner profile, outreach message, enablement assets, tracking requirements, and fraud controls.

prompt_templates.title: Audit And Optimize Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my referral program using these metrics: referral rate [rate], invites per referrer [number], conversion rate [rate], CAC [CAC], LTV [LTV], and fraud issues [issues]. Recommend tests and fixes.

output_examples.input: Design a referral program for a B2B SaaS product with $1,200 LTV and $200 target CAC.
output: Recommended model: double-sided customer referral program.
Reward: product credit or one free month for both parties.
Trigger: ask after onboarding success, renewal, or a positive support interaction.
Tracking: unique links, activation threshold, and delayed payout.
Key metric: referral CAC compared with paid acquisition CAC.

output_examples.input: Help me recruit affiliates for a niche education product.
output: Target partners: newsletter writers, educators, consultants, and creators with audience trust.
Offer: clear flat fee or percentage commission tied to qualified purchases.
Enablement: product overview, sample copy, audience fit notes, and objection handling.
Outreach: personalized message that references specific content and explains audience value.

output_examples.input: My referral program has many signups but few paying customers.
output: Likely issue: weak referred-user conversion after the invite click.
Review the landing page, first value moment, incentive clarity, and signup friction.
Test stronger new-user rewards and show the referrer's endorsement clearly.
Delay reward payout until the referred user reaches a meaningful activation milestone.

best_practices: Use customer value and margin to set rewards before choosing a creative offer.
Ask for referrals at clear moments of customer satisfaction or achievement.
Track referred customers separately so CAC, LTV, churn, and repeat referrals are visible.
anti_patterns: Do not copy a famous referral program without checking product fit and economics.
Do not pay rewards before verifying real activation or purchase behavior.
Do not make the sharing process depend on too many steps or unclear instructions.

faq.question: Can this skill build the referral software for me?
faq.answer: No. It helps plan the program, requirements, metrics, and launch materials. It does not implement tracking software by itself.

faq.question: Does it support both referral and affiliate programs?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers customer referrals, affiliate programs, ambassador programs, and hybrid models.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide product type, audience, LTV, CAC, margin, current referral rate, reward budget, and available tools.

faq.question: Can it recommend reward amounts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can estimate safe reward ranges when you provide customer value, gross margin, and target acquisition cost.

faq.question: Does it include fraud prevention guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers self-referrals, fake accounts, coupon abuse, delayed payouts, verification, and manual review triggers.

faq.question: Is legal or tax review still needed?
faq.answer: Yes. Affiliate terms, payout rules, tax handling, and privacy obligations need review by qualified professionals.
